response.justification: Bogdan is a capable full-stack and distributed-systems engineer, but he is fundamentally mismatched for this role. Professional C++ experience amounts to approximately six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor's thesis project, with no subsequent C++ work of any kind. His primary stack is TypeScript/JavaScript/DLang, and there is no evidence of industrial automation domain knowledge, MQTT or industrial protocol (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850) experience, IEC 62443 or Secure Development Lifecycle exposure, or the systems-level multi-threaded C++ architecture the role demands.
strengths: Strong software engineering fundamentals: TDD, CI/CD, agile, and distributed-systems design consistently demonstrated across senior-level roles
Event-driven and high-throughput architecture experience — Dunnhumby micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O and low-latency patterns
Active knowledge-sharer and mentor: organised recurring refactoring dojos, gave public talks, and delivered multi-day training courses — aligns with the mentoring expectation of the role
concerns: C++ experience is negligible — roughly six months professionally (2014–2015) and a single thesis project; the role requires deep, current C++/STL/OOP mastery as its primary deliverable
No industrial automation, IoT, or embedded-systems background and no exposure to any industrial protocols (MQTT, Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, IEC 61850, DNP 3.0)
Zero evidence of secure development lifecycle practices, IEC 62443 cybersecurity standards, or security-focused engineering — a core non-negotiable requirement
